prompt: We have a Python 2 microservice that was decommissioned in 2019. Nobody who worked on it is still at the company. I need to understand its authentication flow because we're rebuilding something similar. Can you dig through the old repo and tell me how it handled OAuth tokens?

Our legacy billing API was sunset last year but we still get occasional support tickets referencing its error codes. The docs were on a Confluence space that got archived. Help me reconstruct what error codes 4001-4010 meant based on the source code and any remaining artifacts.

There's a Perl CGI script from 2008 that handled our original inventory system. We're migrating to a new platform and need to understand the business rules it encoded, especially around tax calculations for multi-state orders. The original developer retired years ago.

I found an abandoned GitHub repo that seems to implement exactly the graph algorithm I need. The last commit was 6 years ago and the README is sparse. Can you excavate the code and explain the core algorithm, its assumptions, and known edge cases?

We inherited a Rails 3 app from an acquisition. The test suite doesn't run anymore and the deploy scripts reference infrastructure that no longer exists. I need to extract the domain model and business validation rules from the codebase before we rewrite.

Our company wiki has an archived section about the 'Project Phoenix' architecture decisions from 2016. The wiki search is broken but the raw markdown files are in a git repo. Help me piece together why they chose event sourcing and what problems it caused.

There's a deprecated internal CLI tool called 'deploy-magic' that everyone used to use. It was replaced by our CI/CD pipeline but some teams still reference its flags in runbooks. Can you reverse-engineer its flag semantics from the source so we can update the docs?

We have commit messages and PR descriptions from a feature that was built and then reverted 2 years ago. The feature was a rate limiter. I need to understand why it was reverted — was it a design flaw or an operational issue? The engineers who wrote it have all moved on.

An old SOAP API our partners integrated with was deprecated in favor of REST. Some partners still ask about the WSDL schemas. Can you reconstruct the key data contracts from the old WSDL files and map them to our current REST endpoints?

We have a frozen SQLite database from a discontinued mobile app prototype. The schema has no documentation but I need to understand what user data it collected and how sessions were tracked, because legal needs this for a compliance audit.
